text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to a server and receive data back, often in JSON format. Before accessing an API, you usually need authentication—like an API key—to prove your identity. Most APIs have rate limits, meaning you can only make a certain number of requests per minute. If you exceed this, the server might block you temporarily. Error handling is important too; always check the response status codes (like 404 or 500) to know if something went wrong. With practice, you can build applications that reliably fetch and send data using these principles.
